explanation: This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will provide a reason (provideReason). This directly captures the requirement that the robot should explain why it aborted, implying the explanation will occur at some point after the abort.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will provide an explanation (explain). This is a slightly more general interpretation of 'explain why' compared to 'provideReason', but still highly relevant.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will justify its decision (justifyDecision). 'Justify decision' is a strong synonym for 'explain why' in this context.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal). This interprets 'explain why' as explaining the specific circumstance that led to the abort, assuming the abort is an off-normal condition.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then in the next step (X) it will provide a reason (provideReason). This imposes a stricter immediate requirement for the explanation compared to the 'eventually' (F) operator.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then in the next step (X) it will provide an explanation (explain). Similar to the previous, but using the more general 'explain' proposition.
This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then in the next step (X) it will justify its decision (justifyDecision). This requires an immediate justification.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then in the next step (X) it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal). This is a strong temporal constraint for explaining the specific cause.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then it will provide a reason (provideReason) at the same time. While 'should' implies a future action, simultaneous explanation is a possible, though less common, interpretation.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then it will provide an explanation (explain) at the same time. This is a simultaneous requirement with the general 'explain' proposition.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then it will justify its decision (justifyDecision) at the same time. This is a simultaneous requirement for justification.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal) at the same time. This requires immediate explanation of the specific cause.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will provide a reason (provideReason) and that reason will be detailed (detailed). This adds a qualitative requirement to the explanation.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain (explain) and that explanation will be detailed (detailed). This adds a qualitative requirement to the general explanation.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will justify its decision (justifyDecision) and that justification will be detailed (detailed). This adds a qualitative requirement to the justification.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal) and that explanation will be detailed (detailed). This specifies a detailed explanation for the underlying cause.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will provide a reason (provideReason) and that reason will be concise (concise). This adds a different qualitative requirement to the explanation.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain (explain) and that explanation will be concise (concise). This specifies a concise general explanation.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will justify its decision (justifyDecision) and that justification will be concise (concise). This specifies a concise justification.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal) and that explanation will be concise (concise). This specifies a concise explanation for the underlying cause.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will provide a reason (provideReason) and diagnose (diagnose). This implies the explanation includes a diagnostic step to determine the cause.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain (explain) and diagnose (diagnose). This combines a general explanation with a diagnostic action.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will justify its decision (justifyDecision) and diagnose (diagnose). This combines justification with a diagnostic action.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal) and diagnose (diagnose). This means the explanation of the off-normal condition is part of a diagnostic process.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will provide a reason (provideReason) and a problem will have been detected (problemDetected). This suggests the reason provided is related to a detected problem.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain (explain) and a problem will have been detected (problemDetected). This implies the explanation is about the detected problem.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will justify its decision (justifyDecision) and a problem will have been detected (problemDetected). This implies the justification relates to a detected problem.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal) and a problem will have been detected (problemDetected). This specifies explaining the off-normal condition which is also a detected problem.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will provide a reason (provideReason) and a failure will have been detected (failureDetected). This suggests the reason provided is related to a detected failure.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain (explain) and a failure will have been detected (failureDetected). This implies the explanation is about the detected failure.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will justify its decision (justifyDecision) and a failure will have been detected (failureDetected). This implies the justification relates to a detected failure.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal) and a failure will have been detected (failureDetected). This specifies explaining the off-normal condition which is also a detected failure.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will provide a reason (provideReason) for unexpected behavior (unexpectedBehaviour). This suggests the reason provided is tied to an unexpected action.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain (explain) unexpected behavior (unexpectedBehaviour). This implies the explanation focuses on the deviation from expected behavior.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will justify its decision (justifyDecision) due to unexpected behavior (unexpectedBehaviour). This implies the justification is about the unexpected behavior.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal) which is also an unexpected behavior (unexpectedBehaviour). This provides a detailed explanation for the cause.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will provide a reason (provideReason) for an unexpected condition (unexpectedCondition). This suggests the reason provided is tied to an unexpected event.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain (explain) an unexpected condition (unexpectedCondition). This implies the explanation focuses on an unexpected event.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will justify its decision (justifyDecision) due to an unexpected condition (unexpectedCondition). This implies the justification is about the unexpected event.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal) which is also an unexpected condition (unexpectedCondition). This provides a detailed explanation for the cause.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will diagnose the situation. Diagnosis is a step towards understanding and thus explaining 'why'.
This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then in the next step (X) it will diagnose the situation. This requires an immediate diagnostic action.
This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then it will diagnose the situation simultaneously. This implies immediate diagnosis.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) the system will perform a diagnosis (systemDiagnosis). This is a more general statement about the system's diagnostic action.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then in the next step (X) the system will perform a diagnosis (systemDiagnosis). This requires an immediate system-level diagnostic action.
This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then the system will perform a diagnosis (systemDiagnosis) simultaneously. This implies immediate system diagnosis.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will verify its action (verifyAction) and provide a reason (provideReason). This suggests the explanation is part of an action verification process.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will verify its action (verifyAction) and provide an explanation (explain). This combines action verification with a general explanation.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will verify its action (verifyAction) and justify its decision (justifyDecision). This combines action verification with justification.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot aborts a task (abort), then eventually (F) it will verify its action (verifyAction) and explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal). This combines action verification with explaining the specific cause of the abort.
